description: Renaming, dropping, or retyping a column in place on a shared table, breaking every downstream consumer at once.
body: Failure mode: a column is renamed or its type changed directly, so all jobs and queries expecting the old shape fail or silently misread on the next run. Detection signals: downstream parse/cast errors appear right after the change; a column referenced everywhere vanishes; the migration has no dual-write or transition. Do not mutate column names/types in place on shared tables. Safer fallback: add the new column as nullable, dual-write through a transition, migrate consumers, then drop the old column with a rollback path.
